title: Mobile and landline phone subscriptions per 100 people
caption:
Mobile and landline phone subscriptions per 100 people

This multi-panel line chart tracks mobile and landline phone subscriptions per 100 people from 1960 to 2023 across six countries: the United States, United Kingdom, China, India, Ghana, and Nigeria. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 150 subscriptions per 100 people. Mobile subscriptions are shown in red, landline in blue. In the United States, landline subscriptions rose gradually from around 30 in 1960 to a peak near 70 in the early 2000s, then declined to approximately 30 by 2023; mobile subscriptions began rising sharply after 1990, surpassing landlines around 2005 and reaching over 100 by 2023. The United Kingdom shows a similar pattern: landlines peaked near 60 around 2000 before falling to about 45, while mobile subscriptions climbed steeply after 1995, exceeding 120 by 2010 and stabilizing near that level. In China, both lines remain near zero until the late 1990s; landlines peak around 30 in the mid-2000s then fall below 20, while mobile subscriptions surge past 100 by 2020. India’s landline subscriptions stay below 5 throughout, while mobile subscriptions rise after 2000 to nearly 90 by 2015, then plateau. Ghana and Nigeria show minimal landline activity, with mobile subscriptions in Ghana climbing to over 130 by 2020 before dipping slightly, and Nigeria’s mobile line reaching just under 100 by 2023. Across all panels, mobile subscriptions exceed landlines in the 2010s and 2020s, with the most rapid growth occurring between 2000 and 2015.
